business_ideas.idea: The B2B Shit - irreverent B2B media company mocking TechTarget model
Community platform built on existing user base
WeWork's WeLive/Autonomy model - long-term EV rental subscription

predictions.text: Most wealth paths involve either inheritance, family help, early real estate purchases, or high-paying careers
Scott Painter will successfully reinvent auto industry yet again with Drive Autonomy
Alan Wong-style app creators can reach 100M+ valuation in 8-10 years with no employees

disagreements: Mild: Shaan more skeptical of Adam Neumann credibility; Sam defends his vision as delusional optimism vs fraud distinction
dynamics: Sam drives exploration with unusual businesses; Shaan questions assumptions and provides reality checks. Sam excited by counterintuitive findings; Shaan more measured. Both engaged in genuine debate about entrepreneur psychology.
frameworks: Wealth mystery box—most people don't see how wealth works or number of paths available
Bitter vs inspired reaction to others' success—separates winners from complainers
Fog-lifting framework—collecting data points to demystify wealth creation
HOV lane concept—compounding skills from years of work enabling fast wins
learnings: In expensive cities, real estate bought 10+ years ago is the primary lever for current wealth, not new income
Most expensive neighborhood residents are teachers/artists—family money or inheritance is often the missing piece
To save $10M while spending $25k/month in NYC takes 15+ years of $1.5M annual income
B2B lead generation business model ($400-500M revenue from 3,000 customers) operates in plain sight unnoticed
TechTarget profits by tracking job titles across websites and selling their future purchase intent to vendors
Serial CEOs like Scott Painter win by entering massive markets and incrementally improving one problem
Charismatic entrepreneurs ride a knife edge between con artists and world-builders—very similar profiles

banger_quotes.quote: Wealth for most people is a complete mystery unless you grow up with it
If you want to get rich fast, you need examples of people getting rich fast
15% of everyone's gross income goes to mobility—the TAM is just enormous
If he's crazy good or crazy bad, one out of ten who do makes it pays for all losers

numbers_dropped: Brooklyn brownstone: $5M purchase price, $1.1M down payment, $20k/month mortgage + interest
Total annual living expense in high-end Brooklyn neighborhood: $600k-$800k
After taxes: need $1.2M+ annual income to break even at $600k spending
TechTarget: $400-500M annual revenue from ~3,000 customers with market cap $2.1B
Alan Wong: 20M app downloads, verified $100M+ liquid by age 30, built in 8-10 years
Fair raised $300M Series B from SoftBank; Drive Autonomy placed $1B in EV purchase orders
notable: Deep dive into TechTarget exposes how mundane B2B media generates hundreds of millions in revenue while remaining invisible—paradigmatic example of unsexy profitable business
